pysoniq.fourier¶

Minimal-dependency audio processing and fourier transform utilities

pysoniq.fourier.amplitude_to_db(S, ref=1.0, amin=1e-10)[source]¶

Convert amplitude spectrogram to dB scale

Parameters:

  • S (np.ndarray) – Amplitude spectrogram

  • ref (float) – Reference amplitude (default: 1.0)

  • amin (float) – Minimum amplitude threshold

Returns:

db – dB spectrogram

Return type:

np.ndarray

pysoniq.fourier.complex_stft(y, n_fft=1024, hop_length=None, window='hann')[source]¶

Complex-valued short-time Fourier transform; Zxx is a complex-valued matrix that retains magnitude and phase information

Parameters:

  • y (np.ndarray) – Audio time series

  • n_fft (int) – FFT window size

  • hop_length (int or None) – Number of samples between frames

  • window (str) – Window type (‘hann’, ‘hamming’, ‘blackman’)

Returns:

stft_matrix – STFT matrix

Return type:

np.ndarray (complex)

pysoniq.fourier.fft_frequencies(sr=None, n_fft=None, filepath=None)[source]¶

Frequencies corresponding to linear Hz bins.

Parameters:

  • sr (int, optional) – Sample rate in Hz. If None, resolves in order: filepath probe, then module native sr.

  • n_fft (int) – FFT size.

  • filepath (str or Path, optional) – Audio file path. If provided and sr is None, sample rate is extracted.

Returns:

freqs – Frequency array.

Return type:

np.ndarray

Raises:

ValueError – If sr unresolvable (no sr, no filepath, no module native sr), or n_fft is None.

pysoniq.fourier.frames_to_time(frames, sr=None, hop_length=None, filepath=None)[source]¶

Convert frame indices to time in seconds.

Parameters:

  • frames (np.ndarray or int) – Frame indices.

  • sr (int, optional) – Sample rate in Hz. If None, filepath must be provided.

  • hop_length (int) – Hop length in samples.

  • filepath (str or Path, optional) – Audio file path. If provided and sr is None, sample rate is extracted.

Returns:

times – Time in seconds.

Return type:

np.ndarray or float

Raises:

ValueError – If both sr and filepath are None, or hop_length is None.

pysoniq.fourier.frames_to_time_positions(segments, sr=None, hop_length=None, filepath=None, verbose=False)[source]¶

Enrich segment dicts with frame-index bounds derived from time bounds.

Converts ‘start_s’ and ‘end_s’ keys in segment dicts to ‘start_frame’ and ‘end_frame’. Used in UNSEGMENTED mode annotation pipelines to map temporal positions to STFT frame indices for feature slicing.

Parameters:

  • segments (list of dict) – Each dict must contain ‘start_s’, ‘end_s’, and ‘index’ keys.

  • sr (int, optional) – Sample rate in Hz. If None, filepath must be provided.

  • hop_length (int) – STFT hop length in samples.

  • filepath (str or Path, optional) – Audio file path. If provided and sr is None, sample rate is extracted.

  • verbose (bool) – Enable verbose logging.

Returns:

Input dicts augmented with ‘start_frame’ and ‘end_frame’ keys.

Return type:

list of dict

Raises:

ValueError – If both sr and filepath are None, or hop_length is None.

pysoniq.fourier.get_native_sr()[source]¶

Return current module-level native sample rate, or None if unset.

Returns:

Bound native sample rate.

Return type:

int or None

pysoniq.fourier.magnitude_stft(y, n_fft=1024, hop_length=None, window='hann')[source]¶

Real-valued magnitude of short-time Fourier transform (discards phase information)

Parameters:

  • y – numpy array, audio time series

  • n_fft – int, FFT window size

  • hop_length – int or None, samples between frames

  • window – str, window type (‘hann’, ‘hamming’, ‘blackman’)

Returns:

magnitude STFT matrix (real-valued, non-negative)

pysoniq.fourier.mel_filterbank(sr, n_fft, n_mels=128, fmin=0.0, fmax=None)[source]¶

Construct a triangular mel filterbank matrix.

Returns a matrix of shape (n_mels, n_fft // 2 + 1) mapping linear FFT bins to mel-spaced filter outputs. Reference: O’Shaughnessy (1987). Speech Communication. DOI: https://doi.org/10.1016/0167-6393(87)90050-3

Parameters:

  • sr (int)

  • n_fft (int)

  • n_mels (int)

  • fmin (float)

  • fmax (float)

Return type:

ndarray

pysoniq.fourier.mel_frequencies(n_mels=128, fmin=0.0, fmax=11025.0)[source]¶

Frequencies corresponding to mel-spaced bins

pysoniq.fourier.mel_spectrogram(y, sr, n_mels=128, n_fft=1024, hop_length=None, fmin=0.0, fmax=None, window='hann')[source]¶

Compute a mel-scale power spectrogram from a waveform.

Returns linear power shape (n_mels, n_frames) — dB conversion left to caller. Reuses STFT -> power -> mel filterbank pipeline from mfcc, stops before log and DCT.

Parameters:

  • y (ndarray)

  • sr (int)

  • n_mels (int)

  • n_fft (int)

  • hop_length (int)

  • fmin (float)

  • fmax (float)

  • window (str)

Return type:

ndarray

pysoniq.fourier.mfcc(y, sr, n_mfcc=40, n_mels=128, n_fft=1024, hop_length=None, fmin=0.0, fmax=None, window='hann')[source]¶

Compute Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs) from a waveform.

Pipeline: STFT -> power spectrogram -> mel filterbank -> log -> DCT -> n_mfcc coefficients. Returns array of shape (n_mfcc, n_frames).

Parameters:

  • y (ndarray)

  • sr (int)

  • n_mfcc (int)

  • n_mels (int)

  • n_fft (int)

  • hop_length (int)

  • fmin (float)

  • fmax (float)

  • window (str)

Return type:

ndarray

pysoniq.fourier.power_to_db(S, ref=1.0, amin=1e-12)[source]¶

Convert power spectrogram to dB scale

Parameters:

  • S – numpy array, power spectrogram

  • ref – float, reference power (default 1.0)

  • amin – float, minimum power threshold

Returns:

dB spectrogram

pysoniq.fourier.psd(y, sr, n_fft=1024, nperseg=None)[source]¶

Compute power spectral density via Welch method.

Parameters:

  • y (np.ndarray) – Audio time series.

  • sr (int) – Sample rate in Hz.

  • n_fft (int) – FFT size for Welch segments.

  • nperseg (int or None) – Length of each segment. If None, defaults to n_fft.

Returns:

PSD vector of shape (n_fft // 2 + 1,).

Return type:

np.ndarray

pysoniq.fourier.real_stft(y, n_fft=1024, hop_length=None, window='hann')[source]¶

Real-valued short-time Fourier transform

Parameters:

  • y (np.ndarray) – Audio time series

  • n_fft (int) – FFT window size

  • hop_length (int or None) – Number of samples between frames

  • window (str) – Window type (‘hann’, ‘hamming’, ‘blackman’)

Returns:

  • stft_matrix (np.ndarray (real))

  • f

  • t

pysoniq.fourier.set_native_sr(sr)[source]¶

Set module-level native sample rate for silent default in sr-dependent functions.

Parameters:

sr (int) – Sample rate in Hz to bind as the native default.

pysoniq.fourier.stft(y, n_fft=1024, hop_length=None, window='hann')[source]¶

Complex-valued short-time Fourier transform; Zxx is a complex-valued matrix that retains magnitude and phase information

Parameters:

  • y (np.ndarray) – Audio time series

  • n_fft (int) – FFT window size

  • hop_length (int or None) – Number of samples between frames

  • window (str) – Window type (‘hann’, ‘hamming’, ‘blackman’)

Returns:

stft_matrix – STFT matrix

Return type:

np.ndarray (complex)

pysoniq
